title: Spironolactone and Hydrochlorothiazide: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text: aking or plan to take while taking spironolactone and hydrochlorothiazide. Your doctor may need to change the doses of your medications or monitor you carefully for side effects. ) the following nonprescription products may interact with spironolactone and hydrochlorothiazide: aspirin and other nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory medications (NSAIDS) such as ibuprofen (Advil Â® , Motrin Â® ), naproxen (Naprosyn Â® ); potassium supplements. Be sure to let your doctor and pharmacist know that you are taking these medications before you start taking spironolactone and hydrochlorothiazide. Do not start any of these medications while taking spironolactone and hydrochlorothiazide without discussing with your healthcare provider. tell your doctor if you have high blood levels of calcium or potassium or have or ever had Addison's disease or other conditions that may cause high blood levels of potassium, or kidney or liver disease. Your doctor may tell you not to take spironolactone and hydrochlorothiazide. tell your doctor if you have or have ever had asthma, systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE, a chronic inflammatory condition), diabetes, or gout. tell your doctor if you are pregnant, plan to become pregnant, or are breast-feeding. If you become pregnant while taking spironolactone and hydrochlorothiazide, call your doctor. if you are having surgery, including dental surgery, tell the doctor
